project.title: Improving glycosylation properties of PglS
project.projectDescription: This work explore the optimisation of PglS to allow the improved production of glycoproteins modified with Streptococcus agalactiae associated capsule glycans.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: LC-MS protein digests: Purified EPA samples were prepared for analysis using in-solution digestion. Briefly, 10µg of each EPA sample resuspended in 20% acetonitrile were reduced/alkylated with the addition of 10mM Dithiothreitol for 1 hour at room temperature followed by the addition of iodoacetamide 40 mM for 1 hour in the dark. residual iodoacetamide was quenched with the addition of 40mM Dithiothreitol for 15 minutes at room temperature. Following reduction/alkylation, samples were diluted 10-fold with 100mM tetraethylammonium bromide pH 8.5 (Sigma) and digested overnight with Trypsin/Lys-C (1/10 w/w Promega) at 37˚C with shaking at 1000rpm. Digests were acidified with formic acid to 0.2% and then cleaned up using C18 Stage 1,2 tips to ensure the removal of particulate matter. Eluted C18 cleaned up peptide mixtures were dried by vacuum centrifugation at room temperature and stored at -20˚C.  Glycopeptide LC-MS analysis: Digested EPA samples were re-suspended in Buffer A* and separated using a two-column chromatography set-up composed of a PepMap100 C18 20 mm x 75 μm trap and a PepMap C18 500 mm x 75 μm analytical column (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Samples were concentrated onto the trap column at 5 μL/min for 5 minutes with Buffer A (0.1% formic acid, 2% DMSO) and then infused into an Orbitrap Fusion™ Lumos™ Tribrid™ Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) at 300 nL/minute via the analytical column using a Dionex Ultimate 3000 UPLC (Thermo Fisher Scientific). 96-minute analytical runs were undertaken by altering the buffer composition from 2% Buffer B (0.1% formic acid, 77.9% acetonitrile, 2% DMSO) to 28% B over 66 minutes, then from 22% B to 45% B over 10 minutes, then from 45% B to 80% B over 2 minutes. The composition was held at 80% B for 3 minutes and then dropped to 2% B over 5 minutes before being held at 2% B for another 10 minutes. The Lumos™ Mass Spectrometer was operated in a data-dependent mode automatically switching between the acquisition of a single Orbitrap MS scan (maximal injection time of 246 ms, an Automated Gain Control (AGC) set to 100% and a resolution of 120k) every 3 seconds and three Orbitrap MS/MS scans of each ion; a stepped collision energy HCD scan (using 15%, 30% and 45% NCE Stepping, maximal injection time of 250 ms, an AGC set to a maximum of 500% and a resolution of 30k), an Orbitrap EThcD scan (NCE 15%, maximal injection time of 250 ms, AGC set to 500%, a resolution of 30k and using the extended mass range setting to improve the detection of high mass glycopeptide fragment ions 85); and a CID scan (maximal injection time of 250 ms, an AGC set to a maximum of 500% and a resolution of 30k). To confirm the absence of doubly glycosylated glycoforms of EPA in WT PglS derived samples parallel reaction monitoring using both HCD and EThcS analysis were undertaken assessing the doubly, singly and unmodified m/z of the peptides CTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCLSWNQVDQVIR, LIGHPLPLRLDAITGPEEECTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCGGR, GFYIAGDPALAYGYAQDQEPDACTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCR, LDAITGPEEECTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCGGR, LDAITGPEEECTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCGGR, LEGGVEPNCTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCKPVR, MSVDPAIADTNCTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCGQGVLHYSMVLEGGNDALK, NVGGDLDPSCTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCSIPDKEQAISALPDYASQPGKPPR and NVGGDLDPSCTGVTQIASGASAATTNVASAQCSIPDKEQAISALPDYASQPGKPPREDLK.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Glycopeptide analysis was undertaken using MSfragger-Glyco within MSFragger (version 22.0). Samples were searched with a Tryptic specificity allowing a maximum of two missed cleavage events and Carbamidomethyl set as a fixed modification of Cysteine while oxidation of Methionine and glycosylation of Serine with HexNAc (204.0866Da) were allowed as variable modifications. The maximum size of considered peptides was increased from 5000 to 7000 and the maximum amino acid length of peptides increased to 70 amino acids to allow the monitoring of large EPA glycopeptides. For HCD searches y, b, Y, y~/b~ (y and b ion with the addition of HexNAc) were allowed. A maximum mass precursor tolerance of 20 ppm was allowed at both the MS1 and MS2 levels. Samples were searched against a custom database containing the EPA sequence. To ensure high data quality assigned glycopeptides were manually assessed and the HCD and EThcD spectra assigned to each unique glycopeptide annotated with the Interactive Peptide Spectral Annotator (http://www.interactivepeptidespectralannotator.com/PeptideAnnotator.html). Extracted ion chromatograms of unmodified peptides and glycoforms of interest were manually extracted and quantification undertaken using the area under the curve of the extracted features within Freestyle (1.7 SP Thermo Fisher Scientific).

references.referenceLine: Edwards RL, McAllister KN, McGuffey JC, Knoot CJ, Hooppaw AJ, Mackel JJ, Zhou B, Robinson LS, Scott NE, Harding CM. Enhancing O-linking oligosaccharyltransferase functionality through directed evolution. J Biol Chem. 2025 302(1):110885

Characteristics[Modification]: glycosylation of serine with HexNAc (204.0866 Da)
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: operated in a data-dependent mode automatically switching between the acquisition of a single Orbitrap MS scan (maximal injection time of 246 ms, an automated gain control (AGC) set to 100% and a resolution of 120 k) every 3 s and three Orbitrap tandem mass spectrometry scans of each ion; a stepped collision energy high-energy collision dissociation (HCD) scan (using 15%, 30% and 45% normalized collision energy Stepping, maximal injection time of 250 ms, an AGC set to a maximum of 500% and a resolution of 30 k), an EThcD scan (normalized collision energy 15%, maximal injection time of 250 ms, AGC set to 500%, a resolution of 30 k, and using the extended mass range setting to improve the detection of high mass glycopeptide fragment ions); and a collision-induced dissociation scan (maximal injection time of 250 ms, an AGC set to a maximum of 500% and a resolution of 30 k).
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: stepped collision energy high-energy collision dissociation (HCD)
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: three Orbitrap tandem mass spectrometry scans
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: A maximum mass precursor tolerance of 20 ppm was allowed at both the MS1 and MS2 levels.
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram]: at 300 nl/min via the analytical column
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: Tryptic specificity allowing a maximum of two missed cleavage events
